diagrams.title: Learning Resource Pyramid for Green IT
diagrams.description: Hierarchical pyramid showing progression from foundational to advanced resources
elements: Base: Textbooks and Core Books (Foundational Knowledge)
Middle: Online Courses, Case Studies, Standards Documents (Applied Learning)
Upper Middle: Journals and Research Papers (Current Research)
diagrams.purpose: Shows students how to progress from basic concepts to advanced research in Green IT

diagrams.title: Resource Evaluation Framework
diagrams.description: Four criteria for selecting quality learning resources
elements: Currency - How recent is the information?
Relevance - Does it align with learning objectives?
Accuracy - Is it peer-reviewed or fact-checked?
diagrams.purpose: Helps students critically evaluate and select appropriate Green IT learning materials

diagrams.title: Multi-Modal Learning Approach for Green IT
diagrams.description: Different learning modalities and their applications
elements: Visual: Diagrams, videos, infographics - concepts and frameworks
Auditory: Podcasts, webinars, lectures - updates and explanations
Reading/Writing: Journals, textbooks, reports - deep knowledge
Kinesthetic: Simulation labs, projects, experiments - practical skills
diagrams.purpose: Guides students to use diverse learning methods for comprehensive understanding

diagrams.title: Green IT Standards Ecosystem
diagrams.description: Interconnected standards and frameworks for sustainability
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how different standards interconnect and apply to Green IT implementations
